heading: Instructions to United States Executive Directors; indigenous people in borrowing country; determination of impact; protection of rights; consultation
label: 262p–4 Instructions to United States Executive Directors; indigenous people in borrowing country; determination of impact; protection of rights; consultation
notes: Codificationis based on section 701 of title VII of H.R. 3750, One Hundredth Congress, as introduced , and enacted into law by .Section 1605 of Pub. L. 95–118Pub. L. 100–202Dec. 11, 1987
DefinitionsThe definitions in apply to this section.section 262p–5 of this title

children.text: to determine, at the time an initial feasibility study is conducted with respect to a proposed project and to the fullest extent possible, the impact such project would have on indigenous people in the borrowing country;
to ensure compliance with loan conditionalities relating to the protection of the rights of indigenous people to lands and resources; and
to consult with indigenous people, and nongovernmental organizations representing indigenous people, at every phase of loan design, planning, implementation, and monitoring.
